evidence.baton.core.terminal-wrap-defaul.evidence: Same positive-path caveat as baton.core.compact-default-reader and baton.core.medium-padded-default-reader: Firefox 151.0.3 completed positive baton exchanges in GitHub WebTransportBrowser runs 26830637957 and 26860542607, while earlier runs 26835514645, 26835807876, and 26836125845 document the closeSession and browserCloseReceived nondeterminism inherited by positive baton scenarios.

evidence.datagrams.server-sized-one.evidence: Same selected-protocol constraint as datagrams.optional-unreliable, datagrams.server-empty, and datagrams.client-empty: GitHub WebTransportBrowser runs 26829426456 and 26842292496 showed Firefox completes the stream/datagram data path through protocol=optional but does not expose a selected WT-Protocol value in this lane.

evidence.baton.core.short-padded-default.evidence: Same positive-path caveat as baton.core.medium-padded-default-reader: GitHub WebTransportBrowser run 26830637957 passed after the standalone smoke stopped gating on the already documented bidirectional bad-chunk diagnostic. Earlier Firefox positive-path evidence is recorded on baton.core.compact-default-reader and datagrams.optional-unreliable. Runs 26835514645, 26835807876, and 26836125845 document the closeSession and browserCloseReceived nondeterminism inherited by positive baton scenarios.

evidence.streams.large-uni-reply-10mb.evidence: Same selected-protocol constraint as streams.large-bidi-echo-10mb: GitHub WebTransportBrowser run 26904158856, Firefox job 79364305271, showed the Firefox compatibility runner appends protocol=optional to the new C201 stream rows.

evidence.reset.browser-abort-bidi.evidence: Same Firefox stream-mode compatibility path as the C201/C202 stream rows, which passed through protocol=optional in WebTransportBrowser runs 26913123737 and 26914421909.

evidence.reset.server-reset-bidi.evidence: Same Firefox stream-mode compatibility path as the C201/C202 stream rows and C203 reset rows, which passed through protocol=optional in WebTransportBrowser runs 26913123737, 26914421909, and 26917564358.

evidence.close.server-immediate.evidence: Same Firefox stream-mode compatibility path as the C201/C202 stream rows and C205 reset/STOP rows, which passed through protocol=optional in WebTransportBrowser runs 26913123737, 26914421909, 26917564358, and 26920504719.

evidence.close.browser-close.evidence: Same Firefox stream-mode compatibility path as the C201/C202 stream rows and C205 reset/STOP rows, which passed through protocol=optional in WebTransportBrowser runs 26913123737, 26914421909, 26917564358, and 26920504719. Existing Firefox positive-path entries document closeSessionReceived nondeterminism in runs 26802216963, 26835514645, 26835807876, and 26836125845.

expected.reason: Firefox 151.0.2 in GitHub Actions reached pico_baton without a usable WT-Available-Protocols value. The Firefox lane uses protocol=optional to exercise the WebTransport data path, so unsupported-protocol negotiation is not claimed for Firefox in this lane.
expected.evidence: GitHub WebTransportBrowser run 26801289699, Firefox job 79008337128, logged Unsupported callback on stream before wt_baton_accept; server trace reached CONNECT path but did not select WT-Protocol.

expected.reason: Firefox 151.0.2 in GitHub Actions rejected the local self-signed WebTransport endpoint before CONNECT when relying only on serverCertificateHashes. The Firefox CI lane temporarily uses WebDriver acceptInsecureCerts to exercise picoquic's data path, so wrong-hash certificate enforcement is not claimed for Firefox in this lane.
expected.evidence: GitHub WebTransportBrowser run 26801130235, Firefox job 79007856552, failed baton smoke with WebTransportError: WebTransport connection rejected; browser=firefox 151.0.2, geckodriver=0.36.0, server log showed no accepted WebTransport CONNECT.

expected.reason: Firefox CI currently uses WebDriver acceptInsecureCerts to exercise picoquic's WebTransport data path because serverCertificateHashes alone rejected the local self-signed endpoint before CONNECT. While that bypass is enabled, this lane cannot claim unsupported certificate-hash algorithm enforcement.
expected.evidence: Same certificate-validation constraint as handshake.reject_bad_certificate_hash: GitHub WebTransportBrowser run 26801130235, Firefox job 79007856552, failed before an accepted CONNECT when relying only on serverCertificateHashes.

expected.reason: Firefox 151.0.2 did not provide a usable WT-Available-Protocols value in CI, so the Firefox lane uses the baton protocol=optional endpoint and does not claim selected WT-Protocol exposure. The same browser accepted invalid WebTransport protocols constructor inputs instead of throwing, did not send a WT_CLOSE_SESSION capsule after the positive baton exchange, did not reliably complete the dedicated browser-close diagnostic in the full core sequence, does not reliably expose the browser-close diagnostic close reason to pico_baton, showed nondeterministic server visibility for the writable bad-chunk diagnostic close-reason marker, and can fail bidirectional writable bad-chunk diagnostic setup with InvalidStateError after sequential sessions, so those are recorded as browser API/protocol gaps.
expected.evidence: GitHub WebTransportBrowser run 26801289699, Firefox job 79008337128, reached CONNECT path but rejected before wt_baton_accept because no WT-Protocol was selected. Run 26801732536, Firefox job 79009682544, completed the baton exchange but recorded protocolConstructor.ok=false because duplicate, empty, overlong, and non-isomorphic protocols did not throw. Run 26802216963, Firefox job 79011192245, completed Firefox baton smoke and core data exchange but recorded server.closeSessionReceived=0. Run 26802432230, Firefox job 79011866106, recorded server.writableBadChunkCloseReceived=false; run 26802593484, Firefox job 79012384032, recorded the same field true. Run 26802934161, Firefox job 79013469332, failed the compact core scenario because streamWritable reported InvalidStateError on bidirectional-setup. Run 26835514645, Firefox 151.0.3 job 79128194446, recorded closeSession.ok=true but failed baton.core.padded-byob only because server.browserCloseReceived=false. Run 26835807876, Firefox 151.0.3 job 79129244033, failed baton.core.padded-byob on closeSessionOk in the full core sequence. Run 26836125845, Firefox 151.0.3 job 79130370168, recorded server.browserCloseReceived=true, so this field is treated as nondeterministic instead of a stable false assertion.

expected.reason: Firefox 151.0.3 in GitHub Actions still uses the protocol=optional endpoint because it does not provide a usable WT-Available-Protocols value in this lane. Run 26835514645, Firefox 151.0.3 job 79128194446, recorded closeSession.ok=true but failed baton.core.padded-byob only because server.browserCloseReceived=false. Run 26835807876, Firefox 151.0.3 job 79129244033, failed baton.core.padded-byob on closeSessionOk in the full core sequence. Run 26836125845, Firefox 151.0.3 job 79130370168, recorded server.browserCloseReceived=true, so this field is treated as nondeterministic instead of a stable false assertion.
